arguments.name: explore-aot
arguments.prompt:
Explore the project in /tmp/sv-080d6ddc5ed44c80bc3bf80ca0064478/before/ to identify all AOT (Ahead-of-Time compilation) compatibility issues. This is a .NET/C# project (Azure.ResourceManager).

Key things to look for:
1. The .csproj file - check if it has `<IsAotCompatible>true</IsAotCompatible>` or `<PublishAot>true</PublishAot>` flags
2. Any use of reflection that would be incompatible with AOT (e.g., `Type.GetType`, `Activator.CreateInstance`, `Assembly.GetTypes`, etc.)
3. JSON serialization patterns - check if `System.Text.Json` source generators are used or if reflection-based serialization is used
4. Any `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` or `[RequiresUnreferencedCode]` annotations that might be needed
5. Check all .cs files for patterns that need `[JsonSerializable]` attributes
6. Check for `MakeGenericType`, `MakeGenericMethod` calls
7. Look at model classes that might need serialization support
8. Check for dynamic code generation patterns

Read ALL .cs files in the project (recursively under /tmp/sv-080d6ddc5ed44c80bc3bf80ca0064478/before/) and report:
- Full file paths and line numbers of any AOT-incompatible patterns
- What specific changes would be needed for each file
- Whether the .csproj needs changes

Focus particularly on:
- Shared/*.cs files
- Common/**/*.cs files  
- Resources/**/*.cs files
- ManagementGroup/**/*.cs files
- The root .cs files
- Extensions/*.cs files
- Properties/*.cs files
